sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
Commodities Markets — Session Overview

Commodities are experiencing broad-based weakness amid dollar strength and shifting Fed expectations, with precious metals and energy leading the decline. The ongoing commodities supercycle faces headwinds from tighter monetary policy outlook and mixed global growth signals.

Precious Metals
Gold trades at $4485.00/oz (-1.21% weekly), pressured by rising real yields and reduced safe-haven demand. Central bank buying remains supportive at current levels. Silver at $75.42/oz (-1.31% weekly) underperforms on industrial demand concerns. Plat

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
inum ($1931.70/oz) and palladium ($1366.50/oz) both decline over 1.20% weekly on automotive sector weakness. Copper rallies to $6.39/lb (+2.20% weekly), signaling resilient infrastructure demand despite broader risk-off sentiment.

Energy
Oil markets face significant selling pressure with WTI at $91.16/bbl (-5.39% weekly) and Brent at $94.41/bbl (-7.96% weekly). OPEC+ production discipline remains intact, but demand concerns from China's economic slowdown weigh heavily. The Brent-WTI spread widens to $3.25/bbl on US inventory builds. Natural gas climbs to $3.00/MMBtu (+3.59% i

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
ntraday) on seasonal demand patterns.

Agriculture
Wheat declines to $627.00/bu (-3.17% weekly) on improved weather forecasts and strong global supplies. Corn at $455.25/bu and soybeans at $1184.50/bu show relative resilience ahead of planting season.

Trade Ideas
LONG: Copper (infrastructure spending), Natural Gas (seasonal demand), Gold (central bank reserves)
SHORT/HEDGE: Brent crude (demand destruction), Wheat (supply normalization)

Key Risks
Escalating Middle East tensions could spike energy prices. Fed pivot toward more dovish stance would support precious metals complex.
